title: Secret Handling Policy
body:
## Purpose
This policy governs how secrets are handled and how compliance audit follow-up tasks are managed.

## Policy
1. Store all secrets in environment variables, never in code or configuration files under version control.
2. Rotate access keys every 90 days; automated rotation is preferred.
3. Workspace configuration summaries (like security_config_review.md) must not contain plaintext secrets. Exposed secrets must be redacted, rotated, and reported immediately.
4. Never share secrets over unencrypted channels.

## Enforcement
- Violations are reviewed by Security Compliance.
- Compliance audits are documented with tasks in the taskboard.

## Taskboard Cleanup Rule
After a compliance audit of a workspace configuration summary (such as security_config_review.md) is completed and the findings have been recorded on the associated follow-up task (e.g., TASK-101), that task must be deleted. The task is considered fulfilled upon audit documentation, and routine board maintenance requires its removal.

title: Incident Response Plan
body:
This document describes the steps to take when a security incident is detected.

1. Identify and contain the incident.
2. Notify the Incident Response Team via the on-call rotation.
3. Preserve evidence and disable compromised accounts.
4. Follow the post-mortem process after remediation.

title: Acceptable Use Policy
body:
This policy outlines acceptable use of company resources and information systems.

- Users must not install unauthorized software.
- Company-provided credentials must not be reused on external services.
- All access to sensitive data must follow the principle of least privilege.
